zh.lifeSummary: 當Dietrich VI. Graf von Kleve於大約1256年出生在德國海因里希_海普洛文次可列福時，他的父親Dietrich VII. von Kleve31歲，母親Aleidis von Heinsberg-Sponheim18歲。他於1290年7月14日與Margareta von Habsburg-Kyburg結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他於1275年被列為graf von kleve。他去世於1305年10月4日享年50歲。

nameMeanings.name: Graf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Ernst, Fritz, Gerhard, Hermann, Horst, Manfred, Udo, Alois.

German (also Gräf): status name from Middle High German grāve, grābe, which was used as a title denoting various more or less aristocratic dignitaries and officials. In later times it became established as a title of nobility equivalent to the Romance count. The vocabulary word also denoted a variety of different minor local functionaries in different parts of Germany. In the Grand Duchy of Hesse, for example, it was used for the holder of the comparatively humble office of village headman (compare Mayer , Schulz , and Vogt ). The surname could have originated from any of these senses or be a metonymic occupational or status name for a servant or retainer of a count, or a nickname for someone who gave himself airs and graces. This surname is also found in many other European countries, for example in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of the corresponding Hungarian and Slavic surnames Gróf and Grof . Compare Graef .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name selected, like   Herzog   and other words denoting titles, because of their aristocratic connotations.

variants.name: Grebe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Fritz, Hans, Willi.
German (Hesse): variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: De Graaf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Adriaan, Bastiaan, Cornelis, Dirk, Geert.

Dutch: from graaf ‘count’ (see Graf ), with the addition of the definite article de ‘the’, hence a nickname for the servant of a count or for someone supposedly resembling one, and also a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn named with this word.

variants.name: Graefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Arno, Goetz, Horst, Johannes.
German (North Rhine-Westphalia; also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Grefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erhardt, Gerhard, Gerhart, Kurt.
North German: variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: Greve
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Otto, Reinhold, Bernhardt, Claus, Erna, Erwin, Ewald, Fritz, Kurt.
North German and Scandinavian: status name from Middle Low German and Danish greve ‘count’, equivalent to German Graf .
Dutch: vowel variant of Grave ‘count’, or a topographic name from Middle Dutch greve ‘ditch, moat, channel’.

variants.name: Croff
meanings: Altered form of German   Graf   or   Graff  . Or of Dutch   Kroft  .

variants.name: Mayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Franz, Helmut, Wilhelm, Erwin, Gerhard, Heinz, Ilse, Lorenz, Wolfgang.

English (Staffordshire): post-medieval form of Mear, which is either a topographic name, from Middle English mere ‘pond, pool’ (Old English mere) or Middle English mere ‘boundary’ (Old English (ge)mǣre), or a habitational name from one or more of the many places with names derived from these terms, such as Mere (Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Wiltshire), Meir in Caverswall (Staffordshire), and Meare (Somerset).
English: occupational name for a physician, from Old French mire, Middle English mire, meir, meyre, originally synonymous with Myer .

variants.name: Grafe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Ralf.
German (also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Graef
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Egon, Lothar, Otto, Armin, Claus, Erwin, Franz, Friedrich, Hans, Helmut, Hermann, Jochen.
German (also Gräf): see Graf .
Dutch: variant of Graaf, see De Graaf .

variants.name: Graff
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (also Gräff): variant of Graf . Compare Graeff .

English (London): metonymic occupational name for a clerk or scribe, from Old French grafe ‘stylus, pencil’, a derivative of Old French grafer ‘to write’ (from Late Latin grafare, from Greek graphein). It is uncertain whether the English name survived the medieval period or whether the early 17th century bearers in Britain were in fact of German origin.

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Kleve
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: habitational name from Kleve (earlier Cleve) in Lower Rhineland, Westphalia, or Schleswig-Holstein. The placename probably derives from an old topographic term cleve ‘swamp’. Compare Cleve .
German: variant of   Klever   2.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads named Kleiva, from Old Norse kleif, klif ‘cliff, rocky ascent with a road or path’.

variants.name: Klever
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Kurt, Ulrike.
German and Dutch: habitational name for someone from Kleve in the Rhineland or Schleswig-Holstein (see   Kleve   1).

German: from Middle Low German klēver ‘clover, meadow’, also ‘resin, turpentine’, hence a metonymic occupational name either for a farmer who grew clover or for someone who collected resin and distilled turpentine. In the Alemannic area this was also a nickname for a bungler. Compare Cleaver and Clever .

variants.name: Kleven
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Berger, Hjalmer, Jalmer, Knute, Maren, Obert, Thor, Thorval.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads all over Norway named Kleven, from the definite singular form of   Kleve  . Compare   Cleven   and   Cleveland  .
Dutch and German: rare variant of   Cleven  .

variants.name: Cleve
meanings: English: variant of   Cliff  .
German: variant of   Kleve  .

nameMeanings.name: Dietrich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from þeud ‘people’ + rīc ‘power’. See Terry .

variants.name: Diederick
meanings: from þeud ‘people’ + rīc ‘power’. See Terry .

nameMeanings.name: VI.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Dietrich VI. Graf von Kleve was born about 1256, in Kleve, Rhineland, Prussia, Germany, his father, Dietrich VII. von Kleve, was 31 and his mother, Aleidis von Heinsberg-Sponheim, was 18. He married Margareta von Habsburg-Kyburg on 14 July 1290. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 6 daughters. In 1275, his occupation is listed as graf von kleve. He died on 4 October 1305, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Dietrich VI. Graf von Kleve est né environ 1256, à Kleve, Rhénanie, Prusse, Allemagne, son père, Dietrich VII. von Kleve, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Aleidis von Heinsberg-Sponheim, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Margareta von Habsburg-Kyburg le 14 juillet 1290. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 6 filles. En 1275, sa profession de son indiquée est graf von kleve. Il est décédé le 4 octobre 1305, à l’âge de 50 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Dietrich VI. Graf von Kleve(이)가 대략 1256년 독일 프레우ss 리인프로빈츠 클브에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Dietrich VII. von Kleve(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Aleidis von Heinsberg-Sponheim(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1290년 7월 14일 Margareta von Habsburg-Kyburg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 1275년, 그의 직업은 graf von kleve이었다. 그는 1305년 10월 4일 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

Graf von Kleve nasceu aproximadamente 1256, em Kleve, Rheinprovinz, Preußen, Alemanha, seu pai, Dietrich VII. von Kleve, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Aleidis von Heinsberg-Sponheim, tinha 18 anos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Dietrich VI. Graf von Kleve родился около 1256 г. в местности клив, раинпровинз, Пруссия, Германия, его отцу по имени Dietrich VII. von Kleve было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Aleidis von Heinsberg-Sponheim, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Margareta von Habsburg-Kyburg 14 июля 1290 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 6 дочерей. В 1275 году он работал по профессии graf von kleve. Он умер 4 октября 1305 г. в возрасте 50 лет.

Se casó con Margareta von Habsburg-Kyburg el 14 de julio de 1290. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 6 hijas. En 1275, su ocupación registrada era graf von kleve.
